email.subject: Daily Journey: Walker Miles on www.skechers.com

review.raw_markdown:
---

# Walker Miles — Skechers.com Mobile Experience Review
**Date:** March 28, 2026 | **Compared to yesterday:** 2/10

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Well, I'll be honest with you — this is rough. I picked up my phone to buy a pair of Slip-ins like I've done a dozen times, and I couldn't get through the front door. The login page went completely blank. The hamburger menu did nothing. I couldn't tap into a product. The cart was a white screen. If I didn't already know the URL for Men's Shoes by heart, I'd have given up after two minutes. The homepage looks nice enough, and the search mostly works, but you can't run a shoe store where a guy can't log in and can't buy anything. This is not a shopping experience — it's a frustrating obstacle course.

---

## 2. Business Impact Score: 3/10

**Marginal improvement from yesterday's 2/10.** The homepage loads cleanly this time, and Men's Shoes is directly accessible by URL. The BOGO deal is prominently shown. But login, mobile navigation, product pages, add-to-cart, and the cart itself are all either blank or broken. That's the entire purchase funnel gone. A loyal Skechers Plus member like me can't even sign in to use my benefits.

---

## 3. What's Working

- **Homepage loaded cleanly.** The BOGO 50% OFF banner hit me right at the top — I noticed it immediately. Good real estate for a deal-conscious guy like me.
- **Popup dismissed without drama.** After the popup cleared, the Skechers Plus "Free Shipping" offer appeared in that sub-banner. That's relevant to me as a member. No fight required to close anything.
- **Men's Shoes page loads and looks solid.** "Hands Free Slip-Ins" is the very first category tile — that's exactly what I'm looking for and it's right there front and center. Good call. 620 results, clear Filter and Sort controls, reasonable text size.
- **Search returns slip-in results.** Typing "slip in" got me 1,017 results. The search engine understood what I wanted.
- **BOGO deal is consistent.** I saw it on every single page that loaded. If this is a real promotion, the visibility is excellent.

---

## 4. What's Weak

- **Login page is a blank white screen.** Completely empty. I tapped the account icon, and nothing happened — no form, no fields, no buttons. I'm a Skechers Plus member. My points, my free shipping, my saved sizes — all locked away behind a broken door. This is the single biggest failure.
- **Mobile menu is dead.** Couldn't open the hamburger menu at all. As someone who navigates menus rather than typing URLs, this cuts off my main way of moving around the site.
- **Product pages never loaded.** Got stuck on the category page. I can see the Hands Free Slip-Ins tile sitting right there and I can't tap into it. That's like walking to the shoe shelf and having an invisible wall stop me.
- **Add to Cart never happened.** No product page, no "Add to Cart" button ever appeared. The whole purchase attempt collapsed.
- **Cart is a blank white screen.** When the system tried to take me to my cart, there was nothing there. Not even an error. Just white.
- **Search shows Women's first.** I searched "slip in" and the first two results shown are both Women's shoes. I'm a 62-year-old man — I want Men's Slip-ins. There's no personalization happening here at all.
- **"GET 20% OFF" pop-in is intrusive.** A dark banner kept sliding up from the bottom of the Men's Shoes page, covering the Filter controls. I can handle it, but it's annoying when I'm trying to use those buttons.
- **No sense that the site knows who I am.** Even on the steps that worked, nothing said "Welcome back, Walker" or showed my member status. Complete strangers and loyal Plus members get the exact same experience.

---

## 5. Recommendations

1. **Fix the login page immediately.** This is a complete failure for returning customers and member-program loyalty. A blank login screen is unacceptable on any device.
2. **Test the mobile hamburger menu weekly.** It's the primary navigation for phone users. If it's broken, the whole site is broken for mobile.
3. **Fix product tap-throughs from category pages.** The invisible element blocking clicks on the Men's Shoes tiles needs a developer looking at it today.
4. **Render the cart.** A blank cart page at the end of a shopping session is the most direct path to losing a sale.
5. **Filter search results by gender when the user is logged in.** A signed-in male member searching "slip in" should see Men's results first by default.
6. **Reduce pop-in frequency on the category page.** The "GET 20% OFF" bar is showing up on top of navigation controls.

---

## 6. Bottom Line

I've been buying Skechers for years. I know their shoes, I trust the brand, and I'm already in their loyalty program. If I can't log in, can't navigate the menu, can't tap a product, and can't see my cart — I'm going to walk into a store instead or buy from someone else. This site is failing its most loyal customers on mobile. Yesterday was a 2/10. Today I'm calling it **3/10** — the homepage and Men's Shoes page are a little better, and search kinda works, but the purchase funnel is completely broken from login through checkout. That's not a mobile experience. That's a placeholder.

---

## 7. Evidence

**Step 1 — Homepage**
What I saw: Clean, bright homepage. BOGO 50% OFF across the top banner. "Spring Style Guide" sub-banner. Family lifestyle photo, "SPRING SHOPPING GUIDE" headline, dark "SHOP NOW" button. Easy to read without zooming. Icons for search, account, bag, and MENU are small but workable. Not personalized — no mention of my account or Plus status. Nothing targeted at men's comfort/slip-ins on first load.

**Step 2 — Dismiss Popups**
What I saw: Same homepage, but the sub-banner switched to "Free Shipping with Skechers Plus Join Now for free." That's relevant to me as a Plus member, even though I wasn't logged in. No intrusive modal popup to fight. This was smooth and easy. One small bright spot.

**Step 3 — Login**
What I saw: A completely blank white screen. Nothing. No form, no fields, no Skechers branding. The system timed out trying to find the email input. From my perspective as a user, I tapped the account icon and the page just... died. Completely unacceptable. I'd assume the site is down and probably call customer service or leave.

**Step 4 — Men's Category**
What I saw: Another blank white screen. The system tried to tap the mobile menu button and couldn't find it. From my perspective, I tapped the hamburger menu icon and nothing opened. The site is navigationally locked on mobile. I'd be completely stuck here.

**Step 5 — Men's Shoes**
What I saw: A proper, working page. "Men's Shoes" heading with a description about comfort and lifestyle. Category tiles showing "Hands Free Slip-Ins" first — exactly what I want — followed by "Athletic Sneakers." 620 results count displayed. Filter and Sort by controls at the bottom. The layout is readable on mobile; text is reasonably sized. The "Hands Free Slip-Ins" placement at the front is good merchandising for my persona. This is the one step that consistently works.

**Step 6 — View Product**
What I saw: Same Men's Shoes category page, but now with a dark "GET 20% OFF X" bar sliding up from the bottom, partially obscuring the Filter button. The system couldn't click through to an individual product — the link was hidden behind another element. From my perspective, I see the Slip-Ins tile, I tap it, and nothing happens. I might try a few more times, get frustrated, and give up.

**Step 7 — Add to Cart**
What I saw: Men's Shoes category page again, this time with a new "Find the Perfect Pair with our Shoe Finder. Get Started" banner and the continuing "GET 20% OFF" bar at the bottom. Never got to a product page. No Add to Cart button ever appeared. The Shoe Finder feature is actually a potentially good idea for someone like me who cares about comfort tech, but I never got to test it because I'm stuck on the category page.

**Step 8 — View Cart**
What I saw: Blank white screen. Complete page failure. I never actually added anything to the cart (since the product page was inaccessible), but even navigating to the cart URL directly returned nothing. If I somehow got something in my cart through another path, I'd open the cart and see white. I'd think I lost my items.

**Step 9 — Search**
What I saw: Search results page for "SLIP IN" — 1,017 results shown. The heading reads "SLIP IN" and result count "(1017)" is visible below. Filter and Sort by controls are present. However, the first two visible products are both labeled **Women's** — "Skechers Slip-ins: Arch Fit Arcade - See Ya There" and "Skechers Slip-ins: Contour Foam - Cozy Fit." As a 62-year-old man shopping for myself, seeing Women's shoes first is disorienting. There's no gender filter applied by default even though it could reasonably infer my preference. The results are slip-in relevant, which is good, but the Women's-first ordering would send me scrolling or bouncing.

---

## Technical Audit

## Technical Summary

The audit covers 4 steps: Homepage, Men's Shoes category, View Cart, and Search. Two critical infrastructure issues dominate: the View Cart page is being intercepted by **Kasada bot protection** (serving a JS challenge page instead of cart content), and all 5 network/console errors are HTTP 429s from that same bot detection system. LCP and CLS were not captured on any step, indicating incomplete Web Vitals instrumentation.

---

## Accessibility

**21 violations across 4 pages. Severity breakdown:**

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| Critical | 1 |
| Serious | 3 |
| Moderate | 13 |
| Minor | 4 |

**Critical/Serious:**
- **`aria-required-parent` (critical) — Homepage:** ARIA role present without required ancestor role in DOM. Likely a carousel or listitem component rendered outside its required container.
- **`link-name` (serious) — Homepage:** One or more `<a>` elements have no accessible text — no visible text, no `aria-label`, no `title`. Screen readers announce as blank links.
- **`document-title` (serious) — View Cart:** `<title>` element is absent. The View Cart source confirms this: `<!DOCTYPE html><html><head></head>` — the Kasada interstitial page has an empty `<head>`.
- **`html-has-lang` (serious) — View Cart:** Same root cause — Kasada challenge page emits `<html>` without `lang` attribute.

**Structural/Landmark issues (recurring across pages):**
- `landmark-no-duplicate-main`, `landmark-main-is-top-level`, `landmark-one-main`, `landmark-complementary-is-top-level`, `landmark-unique`, `region` — these appear on Homepage, Men's Shoes, and Search. Indicates a deeply nested or duplicated `<main>`/`<aside>` landmark structure, likely caused by CMS template composition or injected widget markup.
- `heading-order` (Homepage) and `page-has-heading-one` (View Cart) — heading hierarchy is broken or entirely absent.

**Minor:**
- `aria-allowed-role` (Homepage, Men's Shoes): Likely `role` applied to elements that don't permit it (e.g., `role="listitem"` on a `<div>` that is itself a child of a non-list element).
- `image-redundant-alt` (Men's Shoes): `alt` text duplicates visible caption text.

---

## SEO

- **View Cart page is un-crawlable.** The Kasada bot protection challenge page is what Googlebot would receive. It has no `<title>`, no `lang`, no `<main>`, no `<h1>`, and no indexable content — only a JS fingerprinting payload.
- **Duplicate GTM/GA IDs in script tags:** Two Google Analytics destination IDs appear side-by-side — `G-XZTV9LQ9DQ` and `G-1234567890` (the latter appears to be a placeholder/test ID that leaked to production). This would cause double-firing of GA4 events and inflated session counts.
- Homepage and Men's Shoes have `lang="en"` on `<html>` — correct.
- No canonical tag issues visible in the truncated source.

---

## Performance

| Page | TTFB | LCP | CLS |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | 34ms | Not captured | Not captured |
| Men's Shoes | 60ms | Not captured | Not captured |
| View Cart | **184ms** | Not captured | Not captured |
| Search | 36ms | Not captured | Not captured |

- **TTFB is acceptable** on 3 of 4 pages. View Cart's 184ms is elevated — likely overhead from the Kasada challenge layer adding a round-trip before serving content.
- **LCP and CLS were not measured on any page.** The instrumentation did not capture these Core Web Vitals. Without LCP, it's unknown whether the above-the-fold image/text paint is within the 2.5s threshold. This is a gap in the audit tooling, not necessarily a site failure — but it needs to be fixed.
- **Third-party script payload is heavy.** Each page loads 14–16 third-party scripts: TikTok Pixel (×2 files), Google Tag Manager, 4 separate GA/Ads destination scripts, Monetate (×2), Pinterest, Bloomreach, QuantumMetric, Reddit Pixel, Yahoo/Yimg (**loaded twice** — duplicate tag on Homepage and Men's Shoes), Bing BAT, Impact.com, Tune, TrustArc. This directly competes with LCP resources on mobile.
- **Duplicate Yahoo script** (`s.yimg.com/wi/ytc.js` appears twice in Homepage and Men's Shoes `<head>`) — wasted request.

---

## Mobile Optimization

- **Viewport height workaround active:** All pages inject `style="--vh: 6.640000000000001px;"` on `<html>` at runtime. This is a JS-based fix for the `100vh` bug in mobile Safari/Chrome where the browser chrome affects the viewport. The raw float value (`6.64px` × 100 = `664px`) confirms it's calculating `window.innerHeight / 100`. This is a known pattern but introduces a layout dependency on JS execution before paint.
- **WebFont loading via WebFontLoader JS** (`webfontloader.js` from cdnjs): This library triggers FOUT (Flash of Unstyled Text) on slow connections. The `wf-*-active` classes on `<html>` confirm it controls font rendering. Modern `font-display: swap` in CSS `@font-face` would be preferable and eliminates the JS dependency.
- No `<meta name="viewport">` visible in truncated source, but the `--vh` JS calculation implies one is present — cannot confirm `width=device-width, initial-scale=1` without full source.

---

## Console & Network Errors

All 5 console errors and all 5 network errors are **HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests)** from the same source:

| URL | Count |
|---|---|
| `/149e9513-.../fp?x-kpsdk-v=j-1.2.291` | 3 |
| `/login/` | 1 |
| `/cart/` | 1 |

**Root cause: Kasada bot protection.** The GUID-prefixed path (`/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/`) is Kasada's challenge infrastructure embedded at the origin. The `fp` endpoint is a browser fingerprinting probe. The 429s indicate the automated browser session was flagged and rate-limited before completing the fingerprint challenge.

This is expected behavior when running automated Playwright/Puppeteer tests against Kasada-protected pages — the bot detection correctly identified the headless browser. However, it means **the View Cart and Login page measurements are invalid** — the page content was never served.

The View Cart HTML confirms this: the source is the raw Kasada challenge page (`window.KPSDK={}; KPSDK.start=...`) with an injected `<script src="/149e9513-.../ips.js">` (Kasada's integrity protection script), not the actual cart.

---

## Recommendations

1. **Fix the audit tooling for Kasada-protected pages.** Cart and Login data is unusable. Options: use a session cookie from a real authenticated browser session, add the test IP/UA to a Kasada allowlist, or use Kasada's official testing bypass if available.

2. **Add LCP and CLS instrumentation.** Without these, the audit cannot assess Core Web Vitals compliance. Use `PerformanceObserver` with `largest-contentful-paint` and `layout-shift` entry types, or ensure the test runner captures them via `page.evaluate()` after `load`.

3. **Remove duplicate `s.yimg.com/wi/ytc.js` script tag** on Homepage and Men's Shoes — one request is wasted.

4. **Remove or replace the test GA4 ID `G-1234567890`** on all pages — this appears to be a placeholder that shipped to production alongside the real ID `G-XZTV9LQ9DQ`.

5. **Fix `aria-required-parent` (critical)** — audit the component that renders the orphaned ARIA role child and wrap it in the correct parent role container.

6. **Add `aria-label` to anonymous `<a>` elements** on Homepage to resolve the `link-name` serious violation.

7. **Fix duplicated/nested landmark structure** (homepage, Men's Shoes, Search) — audit template composition to ensure only one `<main>` exists per page and `<aside>` elements are at top level.

8. **Replace WebFontLoader with `font-display: swap`** in CSS `@font-face` declarations to eliminate JS-gated font rendering on mobile.
